description: The playable class archetypes of Leonoria, organized into class groups. Classes determine starting aptitude bonuses, available skills, starting wealth, starter equipment, and Materium school access. No class is locked by race — but some combinations are mechanically weak or strong.
aptitude_priority_note: Each class lists aptitude priorities from primary to tertiary. These inform the character creation auto-fill and point-buy recommendations.
materium_pool_note: Classes with Materium access begin with a larger Materium pool. Classes without access still have a pool (it supports magical resistance), but a much smaller one.
stamina_pool_note: Stamina is the physical exertion resource for martial and hybrid classes. It is drained when using named martial abilities from attacks_and_spells.json (costs 8–22 per ability). Base attacks never cost Stamina. Recovery: full on Long Rest, half on Short Rest. Pure casters have no stamina_pool_start field. Hybrid classes (Shadowblade, Warden, Aegisbearer, Verdant Warden) have both a Stamina pool and a Materium pool, balanced towards their primary role.

classes.name: Ironguard
classes.description: The backbone of any fighting force. The Ironguard is a disciplined heavy fighter who specializes in endurance, shield work, and heavy armor. They do not rush in — they hold the line until their enemies break.

special_class_trait.name: Last Wall
special_class_trait.description: Once per battle, when Vitality drops below 25%, gain +10% to all physical Hardening for 3 turns.

classes.lore: Trained in organized militaries or by veteran guards, the Ironguard is the most common martial class in Leonoria's settled regions.

classes.name: Battlebrave
classes.description: A fluid, aggressive fighter who thrives in movement and momentum. Where the Ironguard holds, the Battlebrave pushes. Two-handed weapons, fast footwork, and controlled aggression define this class.

special_class_trait.name: Momentum
special_class_trait.description: Each consecutive hit on the same target grants +3% damage (max +15%). Resets if target is switched or missed.

classes.lore: Veterans who found the shield slowed them down. The Battlebrave trusts in offense to outlast defense.

classes.name: Reaver
classes.description: A brutish, rage-fuelled warrior who sacrifices defense for overwhelming offense. Common among Wildmen but occasionally found among battle-hardened Common Folk veterans who have seen too much.

special_class_trait.name: Blood Rage
special_class_trait.description: Below 50% Vitality, all physical damage dealt increases by 20%. Below 25%, increases by 35%. No defensive bonus — pure offense.

classes.lore: Not trained. Forged by violence. The Reaver is what happens when someone survives long enough to stop being afraid.

classes.name: Vanguard
classes.description: A disciplined frontline fighter built around protecting allies. The Vanguard does not kill fastest — they keep others alive long enough to. Their presence on a battlefield is felt most by those standing behind them.

special_class_trait.name: Hold the Line
special_class_trait.description: Allies standing directly behind the Vanguard gain +10% damage reduction while the Vanguard has a shield equipped.

classes.lore: Every army needs someone who faces the worst of it so others don't have to. The Vanguard made that choice deliberately.

classes.name: Duelist
classes.description: A master of one-on-one combat. The Duelist reads opponents, exploits openings, and wins through precision rather than brute force. They fight light and fast, turning the enemy's aggression back on them.

special_class_trait.name: Riposte
special_class_trait.description: After a successful parry, the Duelist may immediately make a counterattack with +20% damage bonus. Once per combat round.

classes.lore: The Duelist learned that the fight is won before the first swing. Everything after that is confirmation.

classes.name: Warmaster
classes.description: A seasoned commander who fights from the front and leads by example. The Warmaster does not just win — they make everyone around them better. Presence and tactical instinct define the class as much as martial skill.

special_class_trait.name: Warcry
special_class_trait.description: Once per battle, issue a Warcry that grants all allies +5% damage for 3 turns. On coordinated attacks, stacks up to +15%.

classes.lore: The Warmaster has learned that the best weapon is the soldier standing next to you — if they know what you need from them.

classes.name: Wayfarer
classes.description: A self-sufficient wilderness specialist and ranged fighter. Equally at home navigating the Dark Forests or negotiating a trade route. Skilled with bows, survival, and reading the land.

special_class_trait.name: Pathfinder
special_class_trait.description: In wilderness biomes, all movement costs are reduced by 25%. Perception checks for detecting ambushes gain +10%.

classes.lore: The Wayfarer lives between places. Neither city nor wilderness fully claims them.

classes.name: Marksman
classes.description: A patient, calculating ranged fighter who specializes in eliminating targets at extreme distances. Where the Wayfarer ranges widely, the Marksman finds one position and makes it count.

special_class_trait.name: Deadeye
special_class_trait.description: Critical hit chance increases by +10% and critical damage by +15% when attacking targets at long range (3+ hexes).

classes.lore: The Marksman knows that the best fight is the one you win before the enemy knows it started.

classes.name: Beastwarden
classes.description: A ranger who fights alongside a bonded animal companion. The Beastwarden is less about personal combat ability and more about the combined effectiveness of handler and beast — a partnership built over years.

special_class_trait.name: Bonded Companion
special_class_trait.description: The Beastwarden begins play with a permanent animal companion. The companion's stats scale with the Beastwarden's level and Conviction.

classes.lore: The Beastwarden did not tame their companion. They earned them — and there is a difference.

classes.name: Skirmisher
classes.description: A fast, mobile combatant who fires and retreats before the enemy can respond. The Skirmisher never stands still — their tactics are constant pressure, constant movement, and never giving the enemy a fixed target.

special_class_trait.name: Evasive Flow
special_class_trait.description: Movement made immediately after an attack does not trigger attacks of opportunity from the target.

classes.lore: The Skirmisher learned that being hard to hit is worth more than being hard to kill.

classes.name: Shadowblade
classes.description: A martial combatant who integrates shadow arts into physical fighting. Not a mage — a fighter who has learned to move through darkness and strike before being seen.

special_class_trait.name: Shadow Step
special_class_trait.description: Once per battle, teleport to any shadow tile within 3 hexes without triggering attacks of opportunity.

classes.lore: The Shadowblade uses the darkness as a tool, not a home. They chose shadow arts practically — not philosophically.

classes.name: Assassin
classes.description: A cold, methodical killer built around eliminating single targets. The Assassin does not brawl — they wait, identify the moment, and end the fight before it begins. They are specialists in closing the last gap.

special_class_trait.name: Execution
special_class_trait.description: Attacks against targets below 30% Vitality deal +25% bonus damage. Against targets below 15%, this increases to +40%.

classes.lore: The Assassin does not hate their targets. That would make the work personal. It isn't.

classes.name: Thief
classes.description: A quick-fingered opportunist who thrives in chaos and preparation. The Thief is the most socially versatile Rogue — equally at home picking pockets, breaking and entering, or reading a room for the fastest exit.

special_class_trait.name: Opportunist
special_class_trait.description: The Thief gains bonus loot from defeated enemies and can disarm traps instantly without a roll when detected.

classes.lore: The Thief is not defined by what they take. They are defined by how many options they keep open.

classes.name: Saboteur
classes.description: A cunning combatant who prepares the battlefield before the enemy arrives. The Saboteur uses traps, planted devices, and timed detonations to control ground rather than fight for it.

special_class_trait.name: Chain Reaction
special_class_trait.description: When a Saboteur trap triggers, it has a 30% chance to detonate any other Saboteur trap within 2 hexes as a chain reaction.

classes.lore: The Saboteur's philosophy: by the time the enemy knows where you were, you've already won.

classes.name: Warden
classes.description: A martial champion of the Light. Not a healer — a fighter who uses conviction and divine light as weapons. Wardens are often associated with specific faiths, but any strong conviction can fuel the class.

special_class_trait.name: Divine Aegis
special_class_trait.description: While Materium pool is above 50%, all team members gain +5% resistance to Shadow Arts damage types.

classes.lore: The Warden is the answer to the question: what does light do when darkness walks among us?

classes.name: Soulkindler
classes.description: A practitioner of healing, restoration, and divine support. The Soulkindler specializes in Vitalism and Soulkindling magic — repairing what is broken, including Temporal damage. The rarest reliable healer in Leonoria.

special_class_trait.name: Temporal Mend
special_class_trait.description: Only the Soulkindler can reverse the permanent Vitality loss from Temporal (Aged) damage. Costs 40% of Materium pool per restoration attempt.

classes.lore: A Soulkindler once said: 'I do not fight death. I argue with it.'

classes.name: Dawncaller
classes.description: A Cleric who has dedicated themselves specifically to fighting the shadow — undead, void-touched creatures, and those corrupted by dark arts. The Dawncaller's light is not gentle. It burns.

special_class_trait.name: Radiant Purge
special_class_trait.description: All Purelight and Dawncalling damage deals a +20% bonus against undead and shadow-touched entities. Shadow-touched enemies struck by the Dawncaller are briefly banished from shadow tile bonuses.

classes.lore: The Dawncaller does not wait for darkness to recede. They decide when it ends.

classes.name: Aegisbearer
classes.description: A Cleric of fortification and sacred protection. The Aegisbearer channels divine light into physical barriers — shields that glow, wards that hold, and armor that turns blades. They exist to make the faithful impossible to break.

special_class_trait.name: Radiant Bulwark
special_class_trait.description: Shields wielded by the Aegisbearer absorb +20% more damage. This bonus applies to any shield-bearing ally within 2 hexes while the Aegisbearer's Materium pool is above 30%.

classes.lore: The Aegisbearer has decided that faith is not just a feeling. It is something you put between the blade and the person behind you.

classes.name: Scholar
classes.description: A mage who prioritizes understanding over power. The Scholar has access to more Materium schools than anyone except the Elementalist, but at lower potency. What they lack in raw damage they compensate with perfect knowledge — tactical analysis, monster identification, and extraordinary learning speed.
starter_kit: Reference Tome (grants +5 to Arcane Theory starting value)

special_class_trait.name: Rapid Learning
special_class_trait.description: All Erudition-connected skill levels gained in Cognition category are doubled. The Scholar levels theoretical knowledge at twice the standard rate.

classes.lore: The Scholar spent years studying what others only use. That gap — between theory and practice — is their home.

classes.name: Elementalist
classes.description: The most balanced magical class — broad access to the Focused Triad with high Materium affinity. Not specialized enough to be the best at any one element, but versatile enough to handle anything.

special_class_trait.name: Synergy Instinct
special_class_trait.description: Elemental synergy bonuses (e.g. Aquas+Tempest) are automatically calculated and triggered — the Elementalist never misses a synergy.

classes.lore: An Elementalist sees the world as forces in conversation. They learned to join the conversation.

classes.name: Arcanist
classes.description: A mage who channels raw arcane force without the theoretical scaffolding of the Scholar or the elemental discipline of the Elementalist. The Arcanist improvises and overwhelms — sometimes both at once.

special_class_trait.name: Arcane Surge
special_class_trait.description: Each spell cast has a 20% chance to trigger Arcane Surge — the spell is cast a second time at no additional Materium cost.

classes.lore: The Arcanist stopped asking whether the power was stable. They started asking whether the target was still standing.

classes.name: Runescribe
classes.description: A mage who inscribes spells in advance and releases them at will. The Runescribe cannot improvise the way an Arcanist can — but they can unleash devastating prepared effects with instant precision that no other mage can match.

special_class_trait.name: Stored Casting
special_class_trait.description: The Runescribe may inscribe up to 3 spells during preparation phases. Inscribed spells can be released as instant actions in combat, ignoring normal casting time.

classes.lore: The Runescribe believes that the most powerful spell is the one you already cast — you just haven't released it yet.

classes.name: Pyrecrafter
classes.description: A mage who has devoted themselves entirely to Fire mastery. The most offensively focused magical class. Every spell they cast burns. Everything around them, eventually, also burns.

special_class_trait.name: Heatmaster
special_class_trait.description: All Fire damage dealt gains +15%. The Pyrecrafter does not suffer the Ignited status from their own fire. Friendly fire rules still apply.

classes.lore: They did not choose fire. Fire chose them, and they were wise enough to say yes.

classes.name: Stormcaller
classes.description: A Materium mage attuned to wind, atmospheric pressure, and electricity. The Stormcaller is the most mobile magical class — they are always in motion, and they make the air itself a weapon.

special_class_trait.name: Cascade Rider
special_class_trait.description: Tempest cascade effects (the electric secondary strike) trigger at 25% chance instead of 15% for the Stormcaller, and deal +10% bonus damage.

classes.lore: The Stormcaller does not call storms. They were born in one.

classes.name: Geomancer
classes.description: A Sorcerer attuned to earth, stone, and the deep places of the world. The Geomancer fights slowly but devastatingly — shaping terrain, raising barriers, and making the ground itself a weapon.

special_class_trait.name: Stoneform
special_class_trait.description: Once per battle, the Geomancer may enter Stoneform for 2 turns, gaining +25% physical damage reduction and immunity to knockback.

classes.lore: The Geomancer understands something the fire mages don't: the earth was here before you, and it will be here after.

classes.name: Aquorist
classes.description: A Sorcerer attuned to water, ice, and fluid dynamics. The Aquorist controls battlefield movement through environmental manipulation — slowing, flooding, freezing. They are the most defensively creative of the elemental specialists.

special_class_trait.name: Fluid Adaptation
special_class_trait.description: The Aquorist is immune to slow effects and gains +15% movement bonus in water or flooded terrain. Aquas and Aquorism spells ignore movement penalties.

classes.lore: The Aquorist knows that water does not fight. It simply finds the way through.

classes.name: Lifewhisperer
classes.description: A practitioner of Athropium, nature magic, and life-force manipulation. Different from the Soulkindler — the Lifewhisperer works with the natural world as a force, not as a faith. They can harm as easily as heal.

special_class_trait.name: Life Reading
special_class_trait.description: Can assess the exact Vitality and current status effects of any biological creature within 3 hexes at will. No Materium cost.

classes.lore: The Lifewhisperer hears what other mages ignore: the quiet voice of everything that is alive, or was, or will be.

classes.name: Beastcaller
classes.description: A Druid who specializes in summoning and bonding with animals. The Beastcaller does not control beasts — they invite them into a shared purpose. The distinction matters more to the beasts than anyone else.

special_class_trait.name: Primal Bond
special_class_trait.description: The Beastcaller may summon scaling beast companions (wolf, bear, bird of prey) whose stats scale with the Beastcaller's level and Conviction. Multiple weaker beasts or one powerful beast.

classes.lore: The Beastcaller's strength is borrowed — from the wolf, the hawk, the boar. They spend their whole life repaying it.

classes.name: Verdant Warden
classes.description: A Druid who defends rather than attacks — reinforcing the living, rooting out corruption, and turning nature itself into armor. The Verdant Warden is a bulwark of green, slow-burning and impossible to dislodge.

special_class_trait.name: Rooted Defense
special_class_trait.description: When the Verdant Warden does not move during a turn, they gain +20% damage reduction for that turn. While rooted, nearby allies within 1 hex also gain +8% damage reduction.

classes.lore: The Verdant Warden does not guard the forest. They are the forest, briefly deciding to stand somewhere specific.

classes.name: Voidweaver
classes.description: A practitioner of the Malevolent Triad. They deal in entropy, darkness, and void energy. Difficult to play — the instability of Darkvoid requires constant management, and Shadow Arts are politically dangerous in Leonoria.

special_class_trait.name: Void Mastery
special_class_trait.description: The Voidweaver reduces Darkvoid's damage variance from ±20% to ±10%. At Expert shadow_weaving level, variance is removed entirely.

classes.lore: The Voidweaver does not ask where the power comes from. They have decided that doesn't matter.
classes.social_warning: Shadow Arts users face legal persecution in most Midlands cultures. Voidweavers often travel in disguise.

classes.name: Bloodsinger
classes.description: A practitioner of Darkblood magic who uses their own life force as fuel. Every powerful Bloodsinger is a controlled self-sacrifice machine. Exceptionally dangerous offensively; physically fragile.

special_class_trait.name: Pain Tithe
special_class_trait.description: Once per battle, can sacrifice 15% of current Vitality to restore 30% of Materium Connection pool. This Vitality loss cannot be healed in the same battle.

classes.lore: Every Bloodsinger has made the same calculation at least once: how much of yourself is worth spending?
classes.social_warning: Blood magic is illegal in nearly every Midlands culture and heavily persecuted.

classes.name: Dreadbinder
classes.description: A Warlock who has bargained with, bound, or partially merged with a demonic entity. The Dreadbinder's power is real — but it belongs to two of them, and only one is entirely in control at any given moment.

special_class_trait.name: Bound Entity
special_class_trait.description: The Dreadbinder has a persistent demonic companion whose power scales with shadow_connection. At high shadow_connection values, the entity may begin acting semi-independently.

classes.lore: The Dreadbinder asked for power and something answered. They are still not sure whether they got the better end of the arrangement.
classes.social_warning: Demonology is banned in virtually all settled Leonorian regions. Dreadbinders are hunted on sight in most cultures.

classes.name: Dominionist
classes.description: A Warlock who specializes in dominating the minds of others. The Dominionist does not fight enemies — they redirect them. A skilled Dominionist can walk into a hostile camp and leave with an army.

special_class_trait.name: Overrule
special_class_trait.description: Once per battle, the Dominionist may attempt to seize temporary control of an enemy below 40% Vitality. The dominated enemy acts on the Dominionist's turn for 2 rounds.

classes.lore: The Dominionist does not consider what they do to be violence. That philosophical position has never comforted their targets.
classes.social_warning: Mind domination is illegal everywhere and is one of the most feared and persecuted dark arts.

classes.name: Malefactor
classes.description: A Witch specializing in curses, hexes, and lingering afflictions. The Malefactor does not win in a single blow — they poison the well, plant the seed of ruin, and wait for it to grow. Patience is their primary weapon.

special_class_trait.name: Lingering Hex
special_class_trait.description: Curses applied by the Malefactor persist after combat ends, continuing to affect targets for up to 24 hours outside of battle.

classes.lore: The Malefactor has learned that the best revenge is the kind that arrives quietly, long after you've left.

classes.name: Blightweaver
classes.description: A Witch attuned to decay, infection, and spreading corruption. The Blightweaver does not curse individuals — they contaminate groups. One touch can spread through an entire battlefield if left unchecked.

special_class_trait.name: Spreading Decay
special_class_trait.description: Any affliction (rot, blight, infection) applied by the Blightweaver has a 25% chance per turn to spread to a random adjacent enemy.

classes.lore: The Blightweaver does not see rot as destruction. They see it as transformation — the world returning to a simpler state.
classes.social_warning: Blight magic is considered plague-craft by most settled cultures. Blight practitioners are killed on sight in populated areas.

classes.name: Bloodwitch
classes.description: A Witch who uses blood as both a medium of power and a tool of manipulation — not through direct violence, but through influence, passion, and the strange pull that living blood exerts on those who share it.

special_class_trait.name: Crimson Influence
special_class_trait.description: The Bloodwitch can redirect the aggression of a blood-affected target, causing them to focus attacks on a chosen enemy for 2 rounds.

classes.lore: The Bloodwitch does not spill blood carelessly. Every drop spent is an investment — in fear, in debt, in power over those who share a wound.

classes.name: Gravecaller
classes.description: A Necromancer who specializes in raising and commanding the dead in numbers. The Gravecaller does not fight — their army does. Management of undead forces and careful positioning define the class.

special_class_trait.name: Undead Legion
special_class_trait.description: The Gravecaller may maintain up to 4 undead summons simultaneously (most Necromancers max at 2). Each summon is weaker individually, but the combined force is overwhelming.

classes.lore: The Gravecaller does not mourn the dead. They see potential in them.
classes.social_warning: Necromancy is illegal everywhere and among the most feared dark arts in Leonoria. Gravecallers survive by hiding what they are.

classes.name: Soulreaper
classes.description: A Necromancer who drains life force from living targets and converts it into Materium or vitality for themselves. The Soulreaper gets stronger as enemies weaken — a self-sustaining predator in combat.

special_class_trait.name: Essence Drain
special_class_trait.description: Each Soulreaper drain attack that deals damage also restores Vitality to the caster equal to 20% of the damage dealt.

classes.lore: The Soulreaper does not think of what they do as theft. Life is a resource. They have simply found a way to reallocate it.
classes.social_warning: Soul drain magic is the most universally condemned dark art in Leonoria. Soulreapers are killed without negotiation if exposed.

classes.name: Rotforged
classes.description: A Necromancer who combines death magic with blight and decay — raising diseased, infectious undead that spread corruption as they fight. The Rotforged turns the battlefield itself into a contamination zone.

special_class_trait.name: Carrion Constructs
special_class_trait.description: Undead raised by the Rotforged carry an active blight infection. Each time a Rotforged undead strikes a living enemy, there is a 30% chance of spreading a blight affliction.

classes.lore: The Rotforged found that death and decay are not opposites. Decay is just death doing the work slowly, the way it prefers.
classes.social_warning: Rotforged are considered plague-bearers on top of necromancers — they face the most extreme persecution of any dark arts practitioner.
